labels.266: Headers, titles, or bracketed labels that introduce or mark sections of content.
labels.2508: Text boundaries marked by narrative or procedural transitions (arguments, transitions, prohibitions, evidence).
labels.8202: Geographic locations or place names within regions, states, or countries.
labels.8248: Text discussing comparative qualities or reasons why something is preferred or valued over alternatives.
labels.8304: Phrases introducing statements of intent or purpose, typically using "is/are" followed by action verbs or descriptive clauses.
labels.8354: The feature fires on text transitions where a contrasting or additional idea/option follows a main statement or feature description.
labels.8386: Specific dates or date ranges indicating when events occur or are available.
labels.8430: The feature activates on text containing contrasting or alternative options separated by "and" or similar conjunctions.
labels.8475: Tokens at the beginning of phrases or clauses introducing descriptive content or instructions.
labels.8573: Transitions from topic introduction or clause completion to continuation of explanatory or descriptive phrases.
labels.8656: Personal names or identifiers split across token boundaries, often with special characters or diacritics.
labels.10397: Counterfactual conditional statements expressing hypothetical outcomes or alternative scenarios.
labels.10451: Numeric sequences or timestamps embedded within text strings, particularly digits appearing between alphanumeric characters or in time/code formats.
labels.10466: Transitions between sentence/section boundaries marked by punctuation followed by newlines or paragraph breaks.
labels.10565: Hyphenated or compound words/place names split across token boundaries.
labels.10602: Text spanning conjunctions or compound phrases with repeated structure across phrase boundaries.
labels.10603: Phrases indicating a transition or contrast between two clauses or time periods.
labels.10614: Transition words or discourse markers (however, anyway, into, from, and) introducing new clauses or ideas.
labels.10615: Comparative or evaluative statements using "is/are" to assert claims about products, concepts, or entities.
labels.10634: Phrases containing "that" connecting two clauses or ideas.
labels.10684: Parenthetical or descriptive phrases enclosed in or separated by formatting markers like parentheses, angle brackets, or metadata tags.
labels.10693: Phrases emphasizing value, convenience, or practical benefits using colloquial language or casual expressions.
labels.10718: The feature detects date and time expressions (specific dates, months, or timestamps) appearing in document metadata or posting information.
labels.10730: Date or time information in numerical format, often preceded by a month name.
labels.10756: Geographic locations or place names (cities, regions, hotels, streets, landmarks).
labels.12295: Hedging or softening phrases that introduce personal opinions or uncertain statements.
labels.12329: Phrases describing where to find more information about an artist's work or portfolio.
labels.12340: Conversational filler phrases like "Oh," "and" used to transition between thoughts or add emphasis.
labels.12438: Abbreviations or acronyms expanded with their full form in parentheses or descriptive clauses.
labels.12439: Tokens marking transition points or sequential progression within a narrative or instructional flow.
labels.12451: Transitional phrases introducing explanations or next steps in informal text.
labels.12458: Numerical quantities or monetary amounts expressed with commas or decimal points within text.
labels.12506: The feature activates on text spans that are separated mid-word or mid-phrase across the >>> and <<< markers, breaking natural word boundaries.
labels.12512: Clausal or phrasal transitions introducing contrasting or causal explanations between two related ideas.
labels.12606: Phrases describing actions taken or efforts made to achieve a goal or result.
labels.12614: Contrasting or qualifying clauses separated by commas that interrupt a main statement structure.
labels.12628: Numeric values or measurements being split across token boundaries during text generation.
labels.12658: Technical product specifications and equipment names with brand or model identifiers.
labels.12712: The feature activates on commas or conjunctions separating items in lists or enumerations.
labels.12726: Proper nouns and named entities enclosed or highlighted between special markers.
labels.12758: Introductory or transitional phrases that establish context or background information before explaining main content.
labels.12759: Punctuation marks or special characters appearing between words or at text boundaries.
labels.14330: Transitions between related clauses or phrases describing sequential information or organizational details.
labels.14347: Transitional phrases introducing explanatory or consequential information between clauses.
labels.14387: Phrases describing relationships or connections between two people or entities.
labels.14414: The feature fires on phrases connecting two clauses or ideas with a transition or explanatory link between them.
labels.14432: Tokens marking transitions between article content and byline/metadata information.
labels.14450: Transitions between clauses or phrases in lists using "and" as a conjunction.
labels.14484: Dates and times appearing between month/day and year or time stamps.
labels.14526: Superlative or ranking comparisons using "one of the most" or "best" construction.
labels.14560: Comparative or contrastive phrases introduced by "should be" or "are" statements that specify scope or conditions.
labels.14562: Transitions between clauses or phrases indicating a shift in narrative direction or temporal sequence.
labels.14610: Geographic and proper nouns, particularly place names and regional identifiers.
labels.14615: The feature fires on text spans containing "of the" or similar phrases marking transitions between descriptive content and subsequent clauses.
labels.14649: Feature activates on product/ingredient descriptions emphasizing natural, plant-based, or botanical components.
labels.14704: Highlighted text forms promotional or descriptive phrases appearing in business listings, reviews, or advertisements.
labels.14720: Transition phrases introducing new topics or elaborations with "about" or descriptive content following.
labels.14745: Sequences containing coordinating conjunctions or list separators connecting related items or alternatives.
labels.14746: Transitions between conditional or suggestive statements and action-oriented phrases separated by punctuation or line breaks.
labels.14749: Hedging or softening language using repeated words or phrases to express uncertainty or possibility.
labels.14756: Contrasting phrases separated by "and" or conjunctions expressing opposition or comparison.
labels.14770: Punctuation or formatting marks (dashes, periods, colons) separating clauses or sections.
labels.14824: Descriptive adjectives or qualities listed in sequence before a transition word or clause.
labels.14835: The feature activates on text spans describing product specifications, amenities, or technical abbreviations that appear in parentheses or follow descriptive text.
labels.14850: Phrases introducing content updates, navigational instructions, or meta-commentary about posts and websites.
labels.16394: Repeated navigation menu or organizational structure text with duplicate service/category labels.
labels.16412: Phrases using "is/are set to" or future-oriented constructions indicating scheduled events or planned announcements.
labels.16424: Transitions between distinct clauses, phrases, or list items separated by commas or conjunctions.
labels.16425: Transitions between clauses or sentences using temporal/conditional connectors like "after," "when," "and," or punctuation marks.
labels.16444: Editorial or promotional phrases introducing upcoming content, events, or collections.
labels.16478: Phrases expressing contrast or negation between what something is and what it is not.
labels.16482: The feature fires on text contrasting what something is or does versus what it is not, often marked by "but" or negation structures.
labels.16500: Temporal or sequential transitions marked by discourse connectors like "this time," "but," or "however" between clauses.
labels.16508: Contractions or negations marked by apostrophes disrupting word boundaries between the firing tokens.
labels.16516: Phrases that connect or bridge two contrasting or complementary clauses using "have" or similar linking constructions.
labels.16532: Listing items or services separated by commas within a descriptive series.
labels.16560: Transitions between clauses or phrases, often marking shifts from one thought or descriptor to another.
labels.16571: Date patterns in published content, typically month and day or full dates in article headers and timestamps.
labels.16572: Metadata or structural separators like dates, author attributions, and navigation elements.
labels.16584: Transition words or phrases connecting two independent clauses or related ideas.
labels.16598: Transitions between contrasting clauses or statements separated by punctuation or structural breaks.
labels.16664: Geographic locations and place names, often cities or regions within larger contexts.
labels.16668: Transition phrases or discourse markers that connect ideas between sentences or clauses.
labels.16680: Institutional or organizational names and acronyms appearing between prepositions or descriptive phrases.
labels.16687: The feature activates on phrases introducing or describing what someone needs to know, do, or understand about a topic.
labels.16749: Phrases describing extent or foundation using "upon," "of," or comparative language to express magnitude or dependence.
labels.16769: Transitions between contrasting or explanatory clauses using causal or temporal connectors.
labels.16804: Numeric values or measurements immediately followed by descriptive titles or qualifications.
labels.16850: Lists of amenities or features separated by commas.
labels.16870: Tokens describing payment methods or ways to complete transactions (mail, cheque, cash, website link, etc.).
labels.16880: Phrases expressing limitations, constraints, or negative outcomes following conjunctions or transitional words.
labels.16882: Phrases describing difficulty obtaining or capturing good images/pictures.
labels.16884: Transitions between clauses or phrases using commas, conjunctions, or descriptive continuations that bridge related ideas.
